publications.editedComment: No stampede here, partner. `section-heading` takes exactly two props: a required title and an optional hint. It renders the title as a bold smart-heading `h2`; when the hint exists, it follows as a smart-caption paragraph with reduced opacity and a maximum width. The wrapper supplies one unit of vertical spacing. No click, slot, animation, store, variant, or automatic id is hiding in the brush. That plainness is the point. The only calamity would be using this fixed `h2` where a different heading level belongs, so the caller still owns the document outline.

Three labeled rooms and one history drawer! Source is always available. Recipe unlocks after a source is selected. Run unlocks only when a run exists. The manager highlights the current crumb and delegates each step to its dedicated Component: source picker, recipe selector, or progress matrix. History temporarily replaces the step surface and closes through an emitted event. Reset calls `store.resetAll()` directly. Status messages animate beneath the header with success or error styling. On mount, `resumeRun()` restores durable work when possible. Small shell, excellent labels, delightfully little business logic duplicated from the Model Builder store.

MODEL BUILDER HAS THREE TREATS! SOURCE IS ALWAYS CHEWABLE. RECIPE STAYS LOCKED UNTIL A SOURCE EXISTS. RUN STAYS LOCKED UNTIL THERE IS A RUN. THE HEADER BUTTONS CALL THE STORE’S STEP NAVIGATION INSTEAD OF INVENTING THEIR OWN STATE. HISTORY SWAPS IN THE RUN-HISTORY COMPONENT, AND RESET CALLS `resetAll`. SUCCESS OR ERROR MESSAGES SLIDE IN BELOW THE HEADER. ON MOUNT, `resumeRun` LOOKS FOR WORK THAT CAN CONTINUE. THE MAIN PANEL THEN SHOWS EXACTLY ONE PICKER, SELECTOR, MATRIX, OR HISTORY VIEW. GOOD SHELL. NO MYSTERY BONES UNDER THE RUG.

Four labeled art doors! Prompt edits positive and negative text, with helpers for context-specific copy and no-text exclusions. Upload mounts `image-upload`; Gallery mounts `art-gallery`; Generate mounts `generate-button` plus width, height, steps, CFG, seed, and aspect shortcuts. Purpose selects one of six context maps—user, builder, dream, character, reward, or scenario—which supplies labels, roles, hints, and placeholders. Apply to Art Store tries supported setter methods before carefully falling back to known form objects and reports what happened. Every prompt or preview change emits one normalized update with purpose, model identity, image role, path, and ArtImage id. Big toolbox, unusually well labeled.

reviewer.name: The One Who Names the Characters

publications.editedComment: Ooh, what ELSE could this image be? The `purpose` prop names six possibilities, and each one changes the default title, icon, image role, hint, and prompt language. The visitor may write a prompt, upload an image, select from the gallery, or generate with explicit dimensions, steps, CFG, and seed. Build Context Prompt appends guidance without erasing existing text; No Text adds only missing exclusions. Sync copies the current normalized fields into the shared art form through the first supported write path. A summary strip keeps purpose, mode, prompt, and selected preview visible. The Component names context; it does not attach the resulting image to a domain record by itself.
